module_info.docstring:
Attention-Weighted Context Pruning via runtime feedback.

This module provides a feedback loop that learns from agent behavior to
improve retrieval ranking. It tracks which context slices lead to actual
tool calls/edits and uses this signal to prune unused context in future
retrievals.

Key features:
1. Track which slices lead to tool calls/edits (in session state)
2. Build lightweight usage model per-repo
3. Adjust retrieval ranking based on historical usage

Expected impact: 30-40% improvement in retrieval precision over time.

classes.name: SliceUsage
classes.docstring: Record of how a slice was used in a session.

classes.name: UsageStats
classes.docstring: Aggregated usage statistics for a symbol.

classes.name: PruningDecision
classes.docstring: Decision about whether to include a symbol in context.

classes.name: AttentionTracker
classes.docstring: Tracks and learns from context usage patterns.

methods.name: record_delivery
methods.docstring: Record that symbols were delivered to the agent.

methods.name: record_usage
methods.docstring:
Record that symbols were actually used by the agent.

Args:
    session_id: Current session ID
    symbol_ids: Symbols that were used
    use_type: Type of use (tool_call, edit, reference)
    use_context: Additional context (e.g., tool name, edit type)

methods.name: get_usage_stats
methods.docstring: Get usage statistics for a symbol.

methods.name: get_cooccurring_symbols
methods.docstring: Get symbols that frequently co-occur with the given symbol.

methods.name: update_global_popularity
methods.docstring: Aggregate one session's deliveries/usages into global popularity.

methods.name: get_global_popularity
methods.docstring: Return global popularity scores for symbols; missing symbols are 0.0.

methods.name: get_hotspots
methods.docstring: Return top symbols by global popularity score.

methods.name: compute_attention_score
methods.docstring:
Compute attention score for a symbol based on historical usage.

Higher scores mean the symbol is more likely to be useful.
Score range: 0.0 to 1.0

methods.name: prune_candidates
methods.docstring:
Decide which candidates to include based on attention scores.

Args:
    candidates: List of symbol IDs being considered
    budget: Maximum number of symbols to include
    min_score: Minimum attention score to include

Returns:
    List of PruningDecisions for each candidate

methods.name: get_session_summary
methods.docstring: Get summary of usage patterns for a session.

methods.name: cleanup_old_data
methods.docstring: Clean up old delivery records to prevent database bloat.

functions.name: create_attention_reranker
functions.docstring:
Create a reranking function based on attention tracking.

Returns a function that can be used to rerank retrieval results.

functions.name: rerank
functions.docstring:
Rerank candidates based on attention scores.

Each candidate should have a 'symbol_id' key.
Returns candidates sorted by combined relevance and attention score.

functions.name: create_candidate_reranker
functions.docstring:
Create a post-processor that reranks Candidates using attention scores.

Combines 70% original relevance + 30% attention score into a new
relevance value, then re-sorts. Compatible with ContextPackEngine's
post_processors parameter.
